nodes.title: Epic: Tool annotations (readOnly/destructive/idempotent/openWorld)
nodes.description:
Add ToolAnnotations to all 20 registered tools so clients can render and confirm-gate by behavior, closing the destructive-ops safety gap from the tool review.

Spec: MCP 2025-06-18 tool annotations; SDK Tool.annotations (ToolAnnotations).

Model: TDD + validation-gated. 6 phase sub-beads run as a linear blocks-chain (P1→P2→P3→P4→P5→P6); each phase is blocked by the previous.

Epic acceptance criteria (verified at the P6 gate):
  - Every registered tool exposes an `annotations` object.
  - delete_project, delete_environment, delete_test_suite, delete_test_case → destructiveHint:true.
  - search_*, probe_page, get_test_suite_results → readOnlyHint:true.
  - probe_page, check_app_in_browser, trigger_crawl, run_test_suite → openWorldHint:true.
  - create_*/update_* annotated (not readOnly; idempotentHint set where it holds).
  - A test enumerates tools and asserts the annotation invariants (incl. destructive set == the 4 deletes).

nodes.title: [ann] P1 · Detailed design review
nodes.description:
Review the governing MCP spec section(s) and the CURRENT implementation. Capture concrete requirements, constraints, real-world client support, and risks. Deliverable: a design-review note on the epic + enumerated open questions.

Specific work:
  - Inventory all 20 tools; classify each on the 4 hint axes.
  - Confirm SDK 1.27 ToolAnnotations field names; note hints are ADVISORY (clients MAY ignore).

nodes.title: [ann] P2 · Architecture analysis & design
nodes.description:
Analyze approach options, choose one, and produce the technical design: interfaces, type/schema changes, file-level change map, capability negotiation, backward-compat and rollback plan. Design is reviewed/approved before scoping closes.

Specific work:
  - Produce the tool→hints matrix (table).
  - Decide where hints are set (each tools/*.ts build*Tool) and title vs name handling.
  - Confirm change is purely additive (no client breakage).

nodes.title: [ann] P3 · Scoping & test plan
nodes.description:
Decompose into concrete commits and author the TDD test matrix (unit + integration) plus the epic's measurable acceptance criteria. Failing-test NAMES are written here, before any implementation.

Specific work:
  - Author __tests__/tools/toolAnnotations.test.ts spec: presence + per-class invariants.
  - Define acceptance: destructive flagged set, readOnly set, openWorld set.

nodes.title: [ann] P4 · Implementation (TDD)
nodes.description:
TDD: land the failing tests from P3 FIRST, then implement to green. No production code without a preceding test. Existing suite stays green; lint + typecheck/build clean.

Specific work:
  - Write the invariant test (red).
  - Add `annotations` to every build*Tool() in tools/ (green).

(TDD: tests precede implementation.)

nodes.title: [ann] P5 · Documentation
nodes.description:
Update README tool tables, docs/, tool descriptions, CHANGELOG, and AGENTS.md as needed so docs match behavior. Add usage examples.

Specific work:
  - Add a behavior/annotations column to the README tools table; CHANGELOG; note in AGENTS.md.

nodes.title: [ann] P6 · Validation gate
nodes.description:
VALIDATION GATE. Run full `npm test`, lint, and build; verify every epic acceptance criterion is demonstrably met. Gates epic closure.

Specific work:
  - Full suite + lint/build; verify the matrix; (no UI) close gate.

Gate verifies the epic acceptance criteria:
  - Every registered tool exposes an `annotations` object.
  - delete_project, delete_environment, delete_test_suite, delete_test_case → destructiveHint:true.
  - search_*, probe_page, get_test_suite_results → readOnlyHint:true.
  - probe_page, check_app_in_browser, trigger_crawl, run_test_suite → openWorldHint:true.
  - create_*/update_* annotated (not readOnly; idempotentHint set where it holds).
  - A test enumerates tools and asserts the annotation invariants (incl. destructive set == the 4 deletes).

nodes.title: Epic: Structured tool output (outputSchema + structuredContent)
nodes.description:
Promote today's JSON-in-text responses to spec structured output: declare an outputSchema per data tool and return structuredContent, keeping the serialized text block for back-compat.

Spec: MCP 2025-06-18 structured tool output (structuredContent + outputSchema).

Model: TDD + validation-gated. 6 phase sub-beads run as a linear blocks-chain (P1→P2→P3→P4→P5→P6); each phase is blocked by the previous.

Epic acceptance criteria (verified at the P6 gate):
  - Each read/data tool declares an `outputSchema`.
  - Handlers return `structuredContent` conforming to that schema, PLUS a back-compat text block.
  - A validator test asserts structuredContent ⊨ outputSchema for each handler's representative output.
  - isError response shape unchanged.

nodes.title: [struct] P1 · Detailed design review
nodes.description:
Review the governing MCP spec section(s) and the CURRENT implementation. Capture concrete requirements, constraints, real-world client support, and risks. Deliverable: a design-review note on the epic + enumerated open questions.

Specific work:
  - Catalog current response shapes (search_* {filter,pageInfo,...}, probe_page, suite results, CRUD {created/updated/deleted}).
  - Review spec rules: structuredContent + SHOULD include serialized text; client MUST validate when outputSchema present.

nodes.title: [struct] P2 · Architecture analysis & design
nodes.description:
Analyze approach options, choose one, and produce the technical design: interfaces, type/schema changes, file-level change map, capability negotiation, backward-compat and rollback plan. Design is reviewed/approved before scoping closes.

Specific work:
  - Design a buildToolResult(payload) helper emitting structuredContent + text block.
  - Author per-tool outputSchemas (reuse README shapes); set JSON Schema 2020-12 dialect.
  - Pick a validator (check if ajv is already a dep); define isError path.

nodes.title: [struct] P3 · Scoping & test plan
nodes.description:
Decompose into concrete commits and author the TDD test matrix (unit + integration) plus the epic's measurable acceptance criteria. Failing-test NAMES are written here, before any implementation.

Specific work:
  - Test matrix: schema-conformance test per handler; back-compat text-block test; acceptance.

nodes.title: [struct] P4 · Implementation (TDD)
nodes.description:
TDD: land the failing tests from P3 FIRST, then implement to green. No production code without a preceding test. Existing suite stays green; lint + typecheck/build clean.

Specific work:
  - Add conformance tests (red); implement buildToolResult, wire all handlers, add outputSchema to tool defs (green).

(TDD: tests precede implementation.)

nodes.title: [struct] P5 · Documentation
nodes.description:
Update README tool tables, docs/, tool descriptions, CHANGELOG, and AGENTS.md as needed so docs match behavior. Add usage examples.

Specific work:
  - README response-shape + outputSchema docs; CHANGELOG; additive-migration note.

nodes.title: [struct] P6 · Validation gate
nodes.description:
VALIDATION GATE. Run full `npm test`, lint, and build; verify every epic acceptance criterion is demonstrably met. Gates epic closure.

Specific work:
  - Full suite + lint/build; validate every handler's structuredContent against its schema; close gate.

Gate verifies the epic acceptance criteria:
  - Each read/data tool declares an `outputSchema`.
  - Handlers return `structuredContent` conforming to that schema, PLUS a back-compat text block.
  - A validator test asserts structuredContent ⊨ outputSchema for each handler's representative output.
  - isError response shape unchanged.

nodes.title: Epic: Resource links for artifacts (screenshots / HAR / console)
nodes.description:
Return screenshots/HAR/console artifacts as resource_link content (renewable presigned URIs) instead of inlining large base64/URLs, shrinking responses and enabling on-demand fetch.

Spec: MCP 2025-06-18 resource links (resource_link content type).

Model: TDD + validation-gated. 6 phase sub-beads run as a linear blocks-chain (P1→P2→P3→P4→P5→P6); each phase is blocked by the previous.

Epic acceptance criteria (verified at the P6 gate):
  - check_app_in_browser artifacts (HAR, console logs) and probe_page screenshots are available as resource_link items (uri/name/mimeType).
  - Screenshots keep an inline-preview path where useful (decision recorded).
  - Expiry/renewal documented (refetch presigned URLs via search_executions).

nodes.title: [reslink] P1 · Detailed design review
nodes.description:
Review the governing MCP spec section(s) and the CURRENT implementation. Capture concrete requirements, constraints, real-world client support, and risks. Deliverable: a design-review note on the epic + enumerated open questions.

Specific work:
  - Inventory artifact outputs: presigned S3 (check_app_in_browser), base64 PNG (probe_page).
  - Review resource_link semantics (not guaranteed in resources/list; for direct retrieval); note presigned expiry.

nodes.title: [reslink] P2 · Architecture analysis & design
nodes.description:
Analyze approach options, choose one, and produce the technical design: interfaces, type/schema changes, file-level change map, capability negotiation, backward-compat and rollback plan. Design is reviewed/approved before scoping closes.

Specific work:
  - Design: which artifacts → resource_link vs inline image; whether to also implement resources/read (defer to Resources epic); response-size targets.
  - Note: extends buildToolResult from the Structured-output epic if scheduled after it.

nodes.title: [reslink] P3 · Scoping & test plan
nodes.description:
Decompose into concrete commits and author the TDD test matrix (unit + integration) plus the epic's measurable acceptance criteria. Failing-test NAMES are written here, before any implementation.

Specific work:
  - Test plan: assert resource_link items carry required fields; response-size reduction assertion; acceptance.

nodes.title: [reslink] P4 · Implementation (TDD)
nodes.description:
TDD: land the failing tests from P3 FIRST, then implement to green. No production code without a preceding test. Existing suite stays green; lint + typecheck/build clean.

Specific work:
  - TDD then implement in probePageHandler + testPageChangesHandler (and getTestSuiteResults if it returns artifacts).

(TDD: tests precede implementation.)

nodes.title: [reslink] P5 · Documentation
nodes.description:
Update README tool tables, docs/, tool descriptions, CHANGELOG, and AGENTS.md as needed so docs match behavior. Add usage examples.

Specific work:
  - README artifacts section (link fields + renewal); CHANGELOG.

nodes.title: [reslink] P6 · Validation gate
nodes.description:
VALIDATION GATE. Run full `npm test`, lint, and build; verify every epic acceptance criterion is demonstrably met. Gates epic closure.

Specific work:

Gate verifies the epic acceptance criteria:
  - check_app_in_browser artifacts (HAR, console logs) and probe_page screenshots are available as resource_link items (uri/name/mimeType).
  - Screenshots keep an inline-preview path where useful (decision recorded).
  - Expiry/renewal documented (refetch presigned URLs via search_executions).

nodes.title: Epic: Elicitation (delete-confirm + missing-credential prompts)
nodes.description:
Use elicitation/create for human-in-the-loop: confirm destructive deletes and prompt for missing credentials — degrading gracefully when the client lacks the capability.

Spec: MCP 2025-06-18 elicitation; 2025-11-25 enums/defaults/URL-mode. (CLIENT capability.)

Model: TDD + validation-gated. 6 phase sub-beads run as a linear blocks-chain (P1→P2→P3→P4→P5→P6); each phase is blocked by the previous.

Epic acceptance criteria (verified at the P6 gate):
  - delete_* tools request confirmation via elicitation when the client supports it; proceed/abort accordingly.
  - check_app_in_browser/trigger_crawl prompt for missing username/password via elicitation when login is needed.
  - When the client lacks elicitation → current behavior preserved (no hang; documented fallback).

nodes.title: [elicit] P1 · Detailed design review
nodes.description:
Review the governing MCP spec section(s) and the CURRENT implementation. Capture concrete requirements, constraints, real-world client support, and risks. Deliverable: a design-review note on the epic + enumerated open questions.

Specific work:
  - Review elicitation flow (server→client request; primitive-type schema only); detect client capability from initialize result.
  - Assess CI/non-interactive risk (must never hang).

nodes.title: [elicit] P2 · Architecture analysis & design
nodes.description:
Analyze approach options, choose one, and produce the technical design: interfaces, type/schema changes, file-level change map, capability negotiation, backward-compat and rollback plan. Design is reviewed/approved before scoping closes.

Specific work:
  - Design capability gate + maybeElicit() helper; per-tool elicitation schemas (confirm boolean; cred fields).
  - Fallback policy: deletes require explicit confirm arg if no elicitation; creds keep current error. Timeouts/no-hang guarantees.

nodes.title: [elicit] P3 · Scoping & test plan
nodes.description:
Decompose into concrete commits and author the TDD test matrix (unit + integration) plus the epic's measurable acceptance criteria. Failing-test NAMES are written here, before any implementation.

Specific work:
  - Test plan: mock client with/without elicitation; accept/reject paths; fallback path; acceptance.

nodes.description:

Specific work:
  - TDD then implement maybeElicit() + wire delete_* and browser/crawl handlers.

(TDD: tests precede implementation.)

nodes.title: [elicit] P5 · Documentation
nodes.description:

Specific work:

nodes.title: [elicit] P6 · Validation gate
nodes.description:
VALIDATION GATE. Run full `npm test`, lint, and build; verify every epic acceptance criterion is demonstrably met. Gates epic closure.

Specific work:
  - Full suite + lint/build; verify BOTH capability paths (present/absent); close gate.

Gate verifies the epic acceptance criteria:
  - delete_* tools request confirmation via elicitation when the client supports it; proceed/abort accordingly.
  - check_app_in_browser/trigger_crawl prompt for missing username/password via elicitation when login is needed.
  - When the client lacks elicitation → current behavior preserved (no hang; documented fallback).

nodes.title: Epic: Tasks (durable async) — spike / prototype
nodes.description:
Evaluate and prototype the experimental Tasks primitive for long-running tools (check_app_in_browser, trigger_crawl, run_test_suite) behind an experimental flag; produce a go/no-go decision. Do NOT ship-depend.

Spec: MCP 2025-11-25 tasks (EXPERIMENTAL); restructured as an extension in the 2026-07-28 RC.

Model: TDD + validation-gated. 6 phase sub-beads run as a linear blocks-chain (P1→P2→P3→P4→P5→P6); each phase is blocked by the previous.

Epic acceptance criteria (verified at the P6 gate):
  - SDK 1.27 task support assessed and documented (supported or not).
  - A flagged prototype (DEBUGGAI_EXPERIMENTAL_TASKS) maps one long-running tool to task handle + tasks/get polling — OR a written blocker rationale.
  - Default behavior unchanged when the flag is off.
  - A decision record (keep-as-spike / adopt-later / drop) appended to the epic.

nodes.title: [tasks] P1 · Detailed design review
nodes.description:
Review the governing MCP spec section(s) and the CURRENT implementation. Capture concrete requirements, constraints, real-world client support, and risks. Deliverable: a design-review note on the epic + enumerated open questions.

Specific work:
  - Review tasks spec + check SDK 1.27 for task schemas/handlers; compare with current executionId + search_executions polling.
  - Note 2026 RC restructuring risk (experimental→extension).

nodes.title: [tasks] P2 · Architecture analysis & design
nodes.description:
Analyze approach options, choose one, and produce the technical design: interfaces, type/schema changes, file-level change map, capability negotiation, backward-compat and rollback plan. Design is reviewed/approved before scoping closes.

Specific work:
  - Design execution→task mapping; flag-gated + additive; trivial rollback (flag off).

nodes.description:

Specific work:
  - Scope a minimal spike (one tool); test plan for the flagged path; acceptance + decision criteria.

nodes.title: [tasks] P4 · Implementation (TDD)
nodes.description:

Specific work:
  - TDD-style spike implementation behind the flag (default OFF).

(TDD: tests precede implementation.)

nodes.title: [tasks] P6 · Validation gate
nodes.description:

Gate verifies the epic acceptance criteria:
  - SDK 1.27 task support assessed and documented (supported or not).
  - A flagged prototype (DEBUGGAI_EXPERIMENTAL_TASKS) maps one long-running tool to task handle + tasks/get polling — OR a written blocker rationale.
  - Default behavior unchanged when the flag is off.
  - A decision record (keep-as-spike / adopt-later / drop) appended to the epic.

nodes.title: Epic: Resources + resource templates (project/env/execution)
nodes.description:
Expose projects/environments/executions as read-only MCP resources via templates (debugg-ai://project/{uuid}, …), declaring the resources (+ completions) capability — reduces reliance on search_* tools and backs the Resource-links epic.

Spec: MCP core resources + resource templates + argument completions.

Model: TDD + validation-gated. 6 phase sub-beads run as a linear blocks-chain (P1→P2→P3→P4→P5→P6); each phase is blocked by the previous.

Epic acceptance criteria (verified at the P6 gate):
  - resources/list, resources/read, and resource templates implemented for project/environment/execution.
  - `resources` (and completions) capability declared in server capabilities.
  - search_* tools remain (back-compat); docs explain resource vs tool.

nodes.title: Epic: Streamable HTTP + OAuth (hosted / remote MCP)
nodes.description:
Enable an OPTIONAL hosted/remote MCP over Streamable HTTP with OAuth (server as Resource Server) so users connect without local npx; leverages the 2026 RC stateless core for simple deployment. Starts with a product/architecture decision.

Spec: MCP 2025-03-26 Streamable HTTP; 2025-06-18/11-25 OAuth Resource Server + RFC 9728; 2026-07-28 RC stateless core.

Model: TDD + validation-gated. 6 phase sub-beads run as a linear blocks-chain (P1→P2→P3→P4→P5→P6); each phase is blocked by the previous.

Epic acceptance criteria (verified at the P6 gate):
  - Decision/ADR on whether to offer hosted MCP (with scope) recorded.
  - If GO: StreamableHTTPServerTransport selectable via env (stdio stays default); OAuth bearer validation against api.debugg.ai; RFC 9728 protected-resource-metadata endpoint.
  - Stdio path unchanged and remains the default.

nodes.title: [remote] P1 · Detailed design review
nodes.description:
Review the governing MCP spec section(s) and the CURRENT implementation. Capture concrete requirements, constraints, real-world client support, and risks. Deliverable: a design-review note on the epic + enumerated open questions.

Specific work:
  - DECISION review: demand, security model, auth reuse, hosting. Review transport + OAuth specs + stateless-core RC. Produce an ADR (go/no-go).

nodes.title: [remote] P2 · Architecture analysis & design
nodes.description:
Analyze approach options, choose one, and produce the technical design: interfaces, type/schema changes, file-level change map, capability negotiation, backward-compat and rollback plan. Design is reviewed/approved before scoping closes.

Specific work:
  - Architecture: transport selection (DEBUGGAI_TRANSPORT); session vs stateless; token validation integration with existing auth; RFC 9728 metadata; threat model.

nodes.title: [remote] P3 · Scoping & test plan
nodes.description:
Decompose into concrete commits and author the TDD test matrix (unit + integration) plus the epic's measurable acceptance criteria. Failing-test NAMES are written here, before any implementation.

Specific work:
  - Scope the MVP (single-tenant first?); test plan (transport + auth, unit + integration); acceptance.

(TDD: tests precede implementation.)

  - If GO: StreamableHTTPServerTransport selectable via env (stdio stays default); OAuth bearer validation against api.debugg.ai; RFC 9728 protected-resource-metadata endpoint.
  - Stdio path unchanged and remains the default.

nodes.title: Epic: MCP Apps (server-rendered UI) — RC watch / spike
nodes.description:
Research-spike an MCP App that renders probe_page/check_app_in_browser results (screenshots, network waterfall, pass/fail) as sandboxed HTML UI. RC feature with nascent client support — prototype + decision only; NOT default-shipped.

Spec: MCP 2026-07-28 RC: MCP Apps (sandboxed-iframe server-rendered HTML).

Model: TDD + validation-gated. 6 phase sub-beads run as a linear blocks-chain (P1→P2→P3→P4→P5→P6); each phase is blocked by the previous.

Epic acceptance criteria (verified at the P6 gate):
  - RC spec + client-support reality assessed and documented.
  - A prototype UI template renders a real result against a supporting client — OR a written blocker rationale.
  - DebuggAI MCP visual verification of the prototype (per repo UI policy).
  - Decision record (pursue-when-stable / park) appended to the epic.
